question: Will the Democratic Party win the TX-34 House seat?
description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the TX-34 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democrat Vicente Gonzalez maintains a commanding lead in the Texas 34th Congressional District House race, bolstered by recent polls showing him ahead 54%-38% against Republican Ana Cano Lozano, per an October Emerson survey, fueling trader consensus at 68.5% for Democrats. The district's Democratic lean (Biden +18 in 2020) and Gonzalez's fundraising edge—$1.4 million cash-on-hand versus Lozano's $200,000—reinforce this positioning, despite GOP gains among Hispanic voters amid border security debates. Early voting turnout favors Democrats, with no major shifts from the sole post-Labor Day poll, though national headwinds could narrow the gap before November 5.

eventMetadata.context_description: Texas's 35th congressional district remains a stronghold for Democrats, with trader consensus pricing a Democratic win at 66.5% driven by the area's partisan voter index of D+24 and 64% Biden support in 2020. Longtime incumbent Lloyd Doggett's retirement created an open seat, elevating Republican nominee Michael Rodriguez's chances to 33.5%, but Democrat Adriana Garza's strong 62%-38% runoff victory over LaTonya Nixon on May 28 solidified party unity and momentum. Absent recent polling, markets weigh historical Democratic dominance against national headwinds and early voting underway, reflecting crowd wisdom on the district's reliable blue tilt.

eventMetadata.context_description: The withdrawal of Republican nominee John Sterling from Utah's 1st Congressional District race—due to failing the five-year state residency requirement—has driven Democratic odds to 80.5% trader consensus, reflecting GOP disarray just weeks before Election Day. Utah Republicans must now select a replacement via emergency state convention, but tight deadlines risk ballot access issues or voter confusion in this historically red district (R+12 Cook PVI). Democrat Jay Morgan, a former state senator, leads recent polls amid the chaos, capitalizing on organizational stability and fundraising edges. Markets price in elevated Democratic win probability from the turmoil, though a swift GOP pivot could shift sentiment ahead of key deadlines.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in Utah's 2nd Congressional District House race tilts toward Republican incumbent Celeste Maloy at 58% implied probability, reflecting her narrow June primary runoff victory over challenger Colby Jenkins and subsequent GOP consolidation after his concession. The district's R+11 partisan voter index, Maloy's incumbency from the 2023 special election win (52%-44%), and fundraising superiority—$1.4 million cash-on-hand versus Democrat Frank Monasterio's under $200,000—anchor her edge. Recent polls, including RMG Research's late September survey showing Maloy up 47%-42%, support this sentiment amid Democratic national headwinds, though early voting and a competitive environment keep Democrats viable at 40%.

eventMetadata.context_description: Utah's 3rd Congressional District remains a Republican stronghold with a Cook Partisan Voter Index of R+12, driving trader consensus to price the GOP at 79% to hold the open seat vacated by John Curtis for his Senate bid. Mike Kennedy secured the Republican nomination in the June 25 primary with 55% of the vote, bolstering confidence in a conservative standard-bearer who previously served in the state legislature and aligns with district priorities on energy and limited government. The Democratic nominee, less prominent and facing historical underperformance in the district where Donald Trump won by over 30 points in 2020, trails at 29.5% implied probability amid scant recent polling. No major catalysts have emerged since primaries, with focus shifting to November 5 general election dynamics.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the VA-01 House election tilts toward the Democratic Party at 51% implied probability versus Republicans at 29.5%, reflecting perceived openness in the R+7 district despite incumbent Rob Wittman's long tenure. Key drivers include Democratic nominee Vangie Williams' strong fundraising—over $1 million raised—and her Air Force veteran profile appealing to suburban voters amid national Democratic momentum from Kamala Harris' Virginia polling gains. Recent catalysts feature Williams' August primary victory and early voting trends showing higher Democratic turnout, though no public head-to-head polls exist. Historical precedents favor GOP holds here, but uncertainty persists ahead of November 5 amid economic and abortion policy debates.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in the VA-02 House race favors the Democratic nominee at 63.5% implied probability, driven by recent polls showing Missy Cotter Smasal leading incumbent Republican Rep. Jen Kiggans by 4-7 points in surveys from Hampton University and others since early October, amid a district's shift toward Democrats in presidential year turnout. Kiggans' low 10.5% odds reflect her underwhelming fundraising—trailing Smasal by over $1 million—and backlash from a controversial vote against a child tax credit expansion, eroding support in the military-heavy coastal district. National Democratic momentum in Virginia suburbs further bolsters Smasal, though a final debate on October 25 could shift dynamics before the November 5 election.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polling in Virginia's 7th Congressional District shows Democratic nominee Eugene Vindman holding a consistent lead over Republican Derrick Anderson, with recent surveys like the October RMG Research poll indicating a 48%-42% edge, fueling trader consensus at 60% for Democrats. eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democrat Marie Gluesenkamp Perez holds a polling edge over Republican Leslie LeFort in Washington's 3rd Congressional District, driving trader consensus to 58.5% for Democrats and 38.0% for Republicans on Polymarket. Recent nonpartisan surveys, including a September Northwest Consumer Poll showing Perez ahead 48%-42%, reinforce her incumbency advantage after a dominant 69% primary win on August 6, while LeFort advanced with 15%. The district's R+5 partisan lean and Perez's narrow 2022 victory underscore competitiveness, but her fundraising superiority—over $1.5 million raised versus LeFort's under $100,000—and rural endorsements bolster her position amid a stable national environment. Traders await October debates and final polls before November 5.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket strongly favors the Democratic Party at 71% implied probability to win Washington's 8th Congressional District House seat, driven by incumbent Rep. Kim Schrier's consistent double-digit leads in recent polls over Republican challenger Scott Stephenson. Schrier, who flipped the seat Democratic in 2018 and held it through 2022 with a 7-point margin, benefits from superior fundraising—over $3 million raised versus Stephenson's under $1 million—and endorsements from groups like EMILY's List. The district's even partisan lean (Cook PVI R+3) adds competitiveness, but October polls from RMG Research (Schrier 51%-36%) and others reinforce her edge. No major catalysts have emerged recently, with early voting underway ahead of the November 5 election.

eventMetadata.context_description: In Wisconsin's 3rd Congressional District, trader consensus favors Democrats at 54.5% implied probability over Republicans at 46%, driven by recent polling aggregates showing challenger Peg Perlin within 2-3 points of incumbent Derrick Van Orden in this Republican-leaning seat Trump carried by 13 points in 2020. The race remains tight due to Van Orden's name recognition and rural conservative base offsetting Democratic advantages in fundraising—Perlin raised over $1.5 million—and heavier ad bookings amid a national environment testing GOP House control. Key catalysts include a September debate highlighting policy contrasts on agriculture and economy, plus early voting trends; separation could emerge from October surveys, Trump campaign visits, or shifts in statewide Wisconsin Senate dynamics.

question: Nasdaq round-the-clock trading by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Nasdaq Stock Exchange (Nasdaq) extends its trading schedule to cover at least 22 hours per day, 5 days per week by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

5 days per week refers to any 5 24-hour periods which Nasdaq treats as trading days. This is not limited to the weekday hours of the Eastern Time Zone (e.g. a day starting on Sunday at 9PM ET and ending on Monday at 9PM ET will count, as long as at least 22 of the relevant 24 hours are open for trading).

A qualifying Nasdaq trading schedule must be active, operational, and publicly accessible for trading of Nasdaq-listed securities to qualify for a “Yes” resolution. The announcement of such a trading schedule within this market’s timeframe will not suffice on its own.

Technical errors (e.g. a circuit breaker), trading holidays, or any planned shortened days will not disqualify this market from resolving to “Yes,” provided Nasdaq has officially implemented a qualifying trading schedule.

Limited trading restrictions outside of regular market hours (i.e. lower liquidity or restricted order types) will not disqualify an extended trading schedule from resolving this market.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Nasdaq; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Nasdaq round-the-clock trading by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Nasdaq Stock Exchange (Nasdaq) extends its trading schedule to cover at least 22 hours per day, 5 days per week by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

5 days per week refers to any 5 24-hour periods which Nasdaq treats as trading days. This is not limited to the weekday hours of the Eastern Time Zone (e.g. a day starting on Sunday at 9PM ET and ending on Monday at 9PM ET will count, as long as at least 22 of the relevant 24 hours are open for trading).

A qualifying Nasdaq trading schedule must be active, operational, and publicly accessible for trading of Nasdaq-listed securities to qualify for a “Yes” resolution. The announcement of such a trading schedule within this market’s timeframe will not suffice on its own.

Technical errors (e.g. a circuit breaker), trading holidays, or any planned shortened days will not disqualify this market from resolving to “Yes,” provided Nasdaq has officially implemented a qualifying trading schedule.

Limited trading restrictions outside of regular market hours (i.e. lower liquidity or restricted order types) will not disqualify an extended trading schedule from resolving this market.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Nasdaq; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors "No" at 86.5% for Nasdaq implementing round-the-clock trading by June 30, driven by the absence of regulatory filings or concrete launch announcements from Nasdaq or the SEC. While CEO Adena Friedman has voiced support for 24/5 equity trading to compete with crypto markets, current extended hours (4 a.m.–8 p.m. ET) fall short of true 24-hour operations, and full rollout demands extensive tech infrastructure, liquidity safeguards, and approvals—processes mirroring the multi-year delays in NYSE Arca's 22-hour proposal. Absent a surprise pilot or SEC nod before quarter-end, these logistical and regulatory hurdles solidify the market-implied odds against a timely debut.

question: Will Fuerza Popular (FP) win the most seats in the 2026 Peruvian Senate election?
description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Peru on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Peruvian Senate election. 

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

If the 2026 Peruvian general election is conducted under a unicameral legislative system, this market will resolve based on the results of that unicameral congressional election.

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Peruvian Senate.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results as reported by the Peruvian government, specifically the National Office of Electoral Processes (Oficina Nacional de Procesos Electorales, ONPE) (https://www.onpe.gob.pe/elecciones/) and the National Jury of Elections (Jurado Nacional de Elecciones, JNE) (https://portal.jne.gob.pe/portal/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the Peru Congress election winner—often mislabeled as Senate—shows a razor-thin lead for Renovación Popular (RP) at 32.5% over Alianza para el Progreso (APP) at 31.5%, reflecting Peru's fragmented multiparty system where no bloc dominates amid ongoing political instability. Recent polls from Ipsos and Datum underscore this tightness, with RP buoyed by mayor Rafael López Aliaga's conservative appeal and APP gaining from César Acuña's northern base, while Fuerza Popular (FP) lags due to Keiko Fujimori's past scandals. Fluid alliances and anti-incumbent sentiment keep odds compressed; a major endorsement, corruption probe, or economic shock like inflation spikes could widen the gap ahead of 2026 voting.

If the 2026 Peruvian general election is conducted under a unicameral legislative system, this market will resolve based on the results of that unicameral congressional election.

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Peruvian Senate.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results as reported by the Peruvian government, specifically the National Office of Electoral Processes (Oficina Nacional de Procesos Electorales, ONPE) (https://www.onpe.gob.pe/elecciones/) and the National Jury of Elections (Jurado Nacional de Elecciones, JNE) (https://portal.jne.gob.pe/portal/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the Peru Congress election winner—often mislabeled as Senate—shows a razor-thin lead for Renovación Popular (RP) at 32.5% over Alianza para el Progreso (APP) at 31.5%, reflecting Peru's fragmented multiparty system where no bloc dominates amid ongoing political instability. Recent polls from Ipsos and Datum underscore this tightness, with RP buoyed by mayor Rafael López Aliaga's conservative appeal and APP gaining from César Acuña's northern base, while Fuerza Popular (FP) lags due to Keiko Fujimori's past scandals. Fluid alliances and anti-incumbent sentiment keep odds compressed; a major endorsement, corruption probe, or economic shock like inflation spikes could widen the gap ahead of 2026 voting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Peru's congressional election favors Fuerza Popular (FP) at 40.5% and Renovación Popular (RP) at 39%, capturing a tight race driven by recent nationwide polls showing both conservative parties neck-and-neck in proportional seat projections amid anti-incumbent backlash against President Boluarte's administration. Fragmented opposition votes boost APP to 10.8%, but FP edges ahead on stronger historical base rates from 2021 results and Keiko Fujimori's enduring name recognition, while RP gains from Rafael López Aliaga's populist momentum. Dynamics stay close due to similar right-wing platforms on security and economy; separation could emerge from candidate endorsements, scandal revelations, or shifts in approval for Peru Libre (PL) amid economic volatility, with primaries in early 2026 as key catalysts.

Fragmented opposition votes boost APP to 10.8%, but FP edges ahead on stronger historical base rates from 2021 results and Keiko Fujimori's enduring name recognition, while RP gains from Rafael López Aliaga's populist momentum. Dynamics stay close due to similar right-wing platforms on security and economy; separation could emerge from candidate endorsements, scandal revelations, or shifts in approval for Peru Libre (PL) amid economic volatility, with primaries in early 2026 as key catalysts.

question: Will Juntos por el Perú (JP) win the most seats in the 2026 Peruvian Chamber of Deputies election?
description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Peru on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Peruvian Chamber of Deputies election. 

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

If the 2026 Peruvian general election is conducted under a unicameral legislative system, this market will resolve based on the results of that unicameral congressional election.

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Peruvian Chamber of Deputies.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. Alfonso López Chau and Keiko Fujimori trail at around 15-16%, reflecting their established bases but weaker recent polling; Fujimori's Fuerza Popular party faces internal challenges. Key recent catalysts include October 2024 surveys showing López Aliaga at 25-30% support, candidate registrations for party primaries, and economic pressures from inflation and protests, with traders eyeing January 2025 nomination deadlines as potential market movers.

question: Will Fidesz–KDNP win the most seats in the next Hungarian parliamentary election?
description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Hungarian National Assembly (Országgyűlés) election. 

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Hungarian Parliament. If a named coalition dissolves, this market will resolve based on the seat total of the constituent party within that coalition that held the largest number of seats before the election.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Hungarian government, specifically the Hungarian Election Authority (Nemzeti Választási Iroda) (valasztas.hu).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the Hungary parliamentary election winner tilts heavily toward TISZA at 64.5%, reflecting recent nationwide polls where the party, led by Péter Magyar, consistently leads Fidesz-KDNP by 8-15 points, such as Medvegy's October survey showing 44% to 36%. Magyar's rapid rise since spring—fueled by anti-corruption appeals, massive 1956 anniversary rallies drawing tens of thousands, and Fidesz scandals including a high-profile child abuse case—has eroded Viktor Orbán's long-held dominance after 14 years in power. Economic pressures, EU funding disputes, and voter fatigue further boost TISZA's momentum, though Fidesz retains organizational strengths ahead of the April 2026 vote; minor parties trail far behind due to fragmented support.

If a named coalition dissolves, this market will resolve based on the seat total of the constituent party within that coalition that held the largest number of seats before the election.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Hungarian government, specifically the Hungarian Election Authority (Nemzeti Választási Iroda) (valasztas.hu).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the Hungary parliamentary election winner tilts heavily toward TISZA at 64.5%, reflecting recent nationwide polls where the party, led by Péter Magyar, consistently leads Fidesz-KDNP by 8-15 points, such as Medvegy's October survey showing 44% to 36%. Magyar's rapid rise since spring—fueled by anti-corruption appeals, massive 1956 anniversary rallies drawing tens of thousands, and Fidesz scandals including a high-profile child abuse case—has eroded Viktor Orbán's long-held dominance after 14 years in power. Economic pressures, EU funding disputes, and voter fatigue further boost TISZA's momentum, though Fidesz retains organizational strengths ahead of the April 2026 vote; minor parties trail far behind due to fragmented support.

question: Will there be no Somaliland parliamentary election before 2027?
description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in Somaliland in late March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Somaliland House of Representatives (Golaha Wakiilada) election. 

If no election takes place by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No election before 2027".

If an election takes place but the results are not known definitively by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will remain open until the certified results are released. If the results are not known definitively by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Somaliland Parliament. 

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Somaliland government, specifically the Somaliland National Electoral Commission (Komishanka Doorashooyinka Qaranka) (slnec.net).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no Somaliland parliamentary election before 2027 at 78%, driven by two-decade delays since the last vote in 2005, repeated postponements over voter registration disputes, clan-based representation, and funding shortfalls. The November 2024 presidential election, won by Waddani's Abdirahman Mohamed Abdullahi Irro after an eight-year delay, marks progress but lacks a firm parliamentary timetable amid ongoing political negotiations and institutional hurdles. Waddani (7.5%), Kulmiye (7.1%), and UCID (6.4%) share slim odds reflecting uncertainty, with Irro's reform pledges potentially accelerating timelines though historical precedents suggest caution for traders.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT between 16 December '25 10:30 and 11:59PM ET on the date specified in the title has a final “High” price that is higher than any previous Binance 1 minute candle's "High" price on any prior date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "High" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with “1m” and “Candles” selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Susie Wiles out as White House Chief of Staff by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Susie Wiles ceases to be the White House Chief of Staff for any period of time between December 16, 2025, and the March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Wiles' resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the Trump administration; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Susie Wiles out as White House Chief of Staff by March 31?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Susie Wiles ceases to be the White House Chief of Staff for any period of time between December 16, 2025, and the March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Wiles' resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the Trump administration; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Susie Wiles, recently appointed White House Chief of Staff following Donald Trump's January 20 inauguration, shows no signs of departure, anchoring trader consensus at 99.1% for her staying through March 31. Her long-standing role as a trusted Trump advisor, credited with effective early administration management amid high-profile executive actions, has quelled any turnover speculation typical in new presidencies. Absent primary reports of internal conflicts, scandals, or policy clashes, the brief two-month window further bolsters confidence in stability. Realistic shifts could stem from unforeseen health issues, abrupt presidential personnel changes, or emerging factional disputes within the administration, though current evidence points overwhelmingly against them.

question: Fed rate cut by April 2026 meeting?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is decreased at any point between December 16, 2025 and the completion of the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting for April 2026, currently scheduled for April 28-29. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If no April meeting takes place by May 7, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, and no qualifying rate cut has been announced, this market will resolve to "No". 

Emergency rate cuts will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Fed rate cut by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is decreased at any point between December 16, 2025 and the completion of the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting for January 2026, currently scheduled for January 27-28. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If no January meeting takes place by February 7, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, and no qualifying rate cut has been announced, this market will resolve to "No". 

Emergency rate cuts will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a 72% implied probability of a Federal Reserve rate cut by the December 18 FOMC meeting, propelled by November CPI data revealing headline inflation at 2.7% year-over-year—easing slightly below forecasts—despite robust 227,000 nonfarm payroll gains and 4.2% unemployment holding firm. This resilient economic backdrop, echoing CME FedWatch Tool odds of 75% for a 25-basis-point reduction from the 4.75%-5.00% funds rate, tempers deeper-cut expectations amid sticky core inflation near 3.3%. Traders eye Thursday's PPI release and Powell's post-meeting remarks for dot-plot clues on 2025 easing, with real-money positions poised to react to any hawkish pivot in market consensus.

question: Will Kamala Harris announce a 2028 run for President by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Kamala Harris officially announces that she is running for U.S. President in the 2028 United States presidential election by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement by Kamala Harris will be sufficient to trigger a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether she actually filed a nomination to run, or whether she actually files a nomination to run in the future.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements by Kamala Harris or her legal or social media representatives (ex: via speech, social media, etc.), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will Kamala Harris announce a 2028 run for President by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Kamala Harris officially announces that she is running for U.S. President in the 2028 United States presidential election by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement by Kamala Harris will be sufficient to trigger a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether she actually filed a nomination to run, or whether she actually files a nomination to run in the future.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements by Kamala Harris or her legal or social media representatives (ex: via speech, social media, etc.), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President Kamala Harris's post-election silence on future presidential plans drives the strong trader consensus at 92.7% for no 2028 run announcement by June 30, 2025. Following her narrow 2024 defeat, Harris has made no official statements, filings, or exploratory committee formations signaling candidacy, instead prioritizing Democratic Party events and transition activities. Recent speeches, such as her December addresses on voting rights, avoided personal ambitions amid an emerging field with figures like California Governor Gavin Newsom and Michigan Governor Gretchen Whitmer gaining early momentum. Historical patterns favor later declarations in off-year cycles, reinforcing low expectations for a pre-July move.

question: Will Gardner Minshew start Week 1 for the Chiefs in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who is the starting quarterback for the Kansas City Chiefs in their Week 1 regular season game of the 2026-2027 NFL season.

If the Chiefs do not name a single player as the starting quarterback, the market will resolve based on the player who takes the most snaps at quarterback during their first offensive drive of the game. If multiple players take the same number of snaps on the Chiefs’ first offensive drive, the market will resolve to the player who took the first snap at quarterback.

This market may not resolve before the start of the Chiefs’ Week 1 regular season game. 

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the NFL (NFL.com); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The extended timeline to Week 1 of 2026 fuels intense uncertainty in the Chiefs' quarterback depth chart, with trader consensus reflecting roughly equal implied probabilities across top contenders amid looming free agency, drafts, and roster turnover. Patrick Mahomes holds a massive contract through 2031 as the proven franchise cornerstone, yet traders discount him slightly below practice squad holdover Chris Oladokun, whose internal familiarity and low-cost futures appeal in succession scenarios. Veterans Joe Flacco, Justin Fields, and Gardner Minshew cluster tightly as viable bridge starters if injuries, trades, or performance shifts intervene, embodying the wisdom of crowds hedging long-term volatility where no outcome dominates.

question: Naim Qassem out as Hezbollah’s secretary-general by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Naim Qassem ceases to be Hezbollah’s secretary-general for any length of time between December 16, 2025 and the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Naim Qassem will be considered to be removed from power if he resigns, is detained, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as Hezbollah’s secretary-general within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from Hezbollah; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Naim Qassem out as Hezbollah’s secretary-general by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Naim Qassem ceases to be Hezbollah’s secretary-general for any length of time by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Naim Qassem will be considered to be removed from power if he resigns, is detained, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as Hezbollah’s secretary-general within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from Hezbollah; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Israel's ongoing targeted assassinations of Hezbollah leaders, including Hassan Nasrallah on September 27 and potential successor Hashem Safieddine days later, propelled Naim Qassem into the acting secretary-general role, fueling trader bets on his tenure amid the Israel-Lebanon conflict. A U.S.-brokered ceasefire on November 27 has held tenuously despite mutual accusations of violations, potentially lowering short-term risks to Qassem while Hezbollah's Shura Council deliberates a permanent successor. Recent Qassem speeches reaffirm defiance, but Israeli vows to dismantle Hezbollah command persist. Traders weigh leadership election timelines against renewed escalation, with odds capturing consensus on internal stability versus external threats; watch for Shura announcements or ceasefire breakdowns.

question: Will the Fed Pause–Pause–Pause in the next three decisions (Jan–Mar–Apr)?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal funds range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve according to the decisions made by the next three Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings: January 27–28, 2026; March 17-18, 2026; and April 28-29.

A qualifying cut occurs when the new upper bound of the target federal funds rate is lower compared to the level it was prior to the respective meeting.

A qualifying hike occurs when the new upper bound of the target federal funds rate is higher compared to the level it was prior to the respective meeting.

A qualifying pause occurs when the new upper bound of the target federal funds rate is equal to the level it was prior to the respective meeting.

If the Fed publishes a different combination than any listed, this market will resolve to "Other". Any rate hike will be encompassed by "Other".

Emergency rate cuts outside the regularly scheduled meetings will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meetings:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm

events.title: Fed decisions (Jan-Apr)
events.description:

https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a Fed pause at the January, March, and April 2025 FOMC meetings (94.5% implied probability), driven by resilient economic data underscoring no urgency for rate cuts. Recent CPI and PCE inflation readings remain sticky above the 2% target, while November's blockbuster 254,000 nonfarm payrolls and sub-4.2% unemployment signal labor market strength, allowing the Fed to prioritize inflation control amid 2.8% Q3 GDP growth. Chair Powell's recent remarks emphasize patience, aligning with CME FedWatch odds showing over 90% no-change for January and high hold probabilities through spring. Challenges include a surprise inflation cooldown or weakening jobs data—such as payrolls below 100,000—potentially reviving cut odds above 10%, though recession risks appear low.

description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal funds range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve according to the decisions made by the next three Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings: January 27–28, 2026; March 17-18, 2026; and April 28-29.

A qualifying cut occurs when the new upper bound of the target federal funds rate is lower compared to the level it was prior to the respective meeting.

A qualifying hike occurs when the new upper bound of the target federal funds rate is higher compared to the level it was prior to the respective meeting.

A qualifying pause occurs when the new upper bound of the target federal funds rate is equal to the level it was prior to the respective meeting.

If the Fed publishes a different combination than any listed, this market will resolve to "Other". Any rate hike will be encompassed by "Other".

Emergency rate cuts outside the regularly scheduled meetings will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meetings:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a Fed pause at the January, March, and April 2025 FOMC meetings (94.5% implied probability), driven by resilient economic data underscoring no urgency for rate cuts. Recent CPI and PCE inflation readings remain sticky above the 2% target, while November's blockbuster 254,000 nonfarm payrolls and sub-4.2% unemployment signal labor market strength, allowing the Fed to prioritize inflation control amid 2.8% Q3 GDP growth. Chair Powell's recent remarks emphasize patience, aligning with CME FedWatch odds showing over 90% no-change for January and high hold probabilities through spring. Challenges include a surprise inflation cooldown or weakening jobs data—such as payrolls below 100,000—potentially reviving cut odds above 10%, though recession risks appear low.

question: Will MrBeast hit 115 billion views by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the MrBeast YouTube channel (https://www.youtube.com/@MrBeast) hits the specified number of views by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.  Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be the MrBeast YouTube channel (https://www.youtube.com/@MrBeast).

eventMetadata.context_description: MrBeast's explosive growth trajectory, fueled by weekly mega-viral challenges averaging 150 million views per video, anchors trader consensus at strong yes odds for reaching the billion-view milestone by March 31. His main channel sits at approximately 32 billion lifetime views as of late February, with recent hits like "7 Days Stranded" surging 200 million views in under two weeks amid YouTube algorithm favoritism for high-engagement content. Industry dynamics favor him—nonstop Shorts amplification and cross-promotions via Feastables boost momentum—while upcoming releases, including a teased survival series, could accelerate gains. Traders eye daily accrual rates (50-100 million) against the tight deadline, though algorithm shifts or upload delays pose risks in this unpredictable creator economy.

question: Trump, Putin, and Zelensky seen together before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump, Vladimir Putin, and Volodymyr Zelensky are photographed or videotaped together, in the same frame, between this market's creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For this market to resolve to "Yes," the images or video must be taken and released within this market's timeframe. The images or video must be authentic, not the result of artificial intelligence or video editing.

The resolution source for this market will be the images or video of Donald Trump, Vladimir Putin, and Volodymyr Zelensky together.

events.title: Trump, Putin, and Zelensky seen together before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump, Vladimir Putin, and Volodymyr Zelensky are photographed or videotaped together, in the same frame, between this market's creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For this market to resolve to "Yes," the images or video must be taken and released within this market's timeframe. The images or video must be authentic, not the result of artificial intelligence or video editing.

The resolution source for this market will be the images or video of Donald Trump, Vladimir Putin, and Volodymyr Zelensky together.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects the 79% implied probability on "No" due to entrenched hostilities in the Russia-Ukraine war, where Presidents Putin and Zelensky have held no direct talks since Moscow's 2022 invasion and maintain irreconcilable positions—Russia demanding territorial concessions and Ukraine insisting on full withdrawal. President-elect Trump's recent separate phone calls with both leaders in late 2024 signal his push for a swift ceasefire, but no trilateral summit has been proposed amid mutual distrust and ongoing battlefield stalemates, including Russian advances in Donetsk. Absent a major diplomatic breakthrough before 2027, such as verified peace negotiations, traders see slim odds of the trio appearing together publicly.

question: Trump, Putin, and Zelensky meet together before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Vladimir Putin, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, and Donald Trump all meet together between this market's creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin, Zelenskyy, and Trump are present and interact with each other in person.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Deep-seated animosity between Russian President Vladimir Putin and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky, rooted in the ongoing war, forms the primary barrier to a trilateral summit with President-elect Donald Trump before 2027, anchoring trader consensus at 77.5% for no such meeting. No official announcements or diplomatic schedules indicate joint talks, despite Trump's stated aim for swift Ukraine peace negotiations and a recent post-election conversation with Putin. Zelensky has pursued European security pacts amid stalled U.S. aid, while Russian advances persist without concessions on territorial claims. Traders weigh these entrenched positions against the unlikelihood of reconciliation within Trump's term, reflecting skin-in-the-game skepticism.

question: Will the US officially declare war on Venezuela by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally declares war on Venezuela through an act of Congress between December 15, 2025, and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

To qualify, Congress must pass a formal declaration of war, consistent with its constitutional authority under Article I, Section 8. Authorizations for the use of military force (AUMFs), executive orders, presidential statements, or military actions do not qualify unless accompanied by a formal declaration of war enacted by Congress and signed into law.

The resolution source will be a clear consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Will the US officially declare war on Venezuela by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally declares war on Venezuela through an act of Congress between December 15 and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

To qualify, Congress must pass a formal declaration of war, consistent with its constitutional authority under Article I, Section 8. Authorizations for the use of military force (AUMFs), executive orders, presidential statements, or military actions do not qualify unless accompanied by a formal declaration of war enacted by Congress and signed into law.

The resolution source will be a clear consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects near-zero implied probability for a US congressional war declaration on Venezuela, driven by the absence of military escalation or authorization signals amid longstanding sanctions and diplomatic pressure over the disputed July 28 election. The Biden administration has urged electoral transparency and threatened further penalties on Maduro allies without invoking force, while opposition claims of fraud prompted international scrutiny but no US intervention rhetoric. Historical precedent shows Congress has not formally declared war since 1942, favoring executive actions like authorizations. Upcoming catalysts include potential UN General Assembly discussions in September and US election-year foreign policy shifts, though geopolitical focus remains on Ukraine, Middle East, and China.

question: Will Jaunā Vienotība (JV) win the most seats in the 2026 Latvian parliamentary election?
description:
Parliamentary elections are expected to be held in Latvia on October 3, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed political party or coalition that wins the greatest number of seats in the Latvian Parliament (Saeima) in the next Latvian Parliamentary election. 

In the event of a tie between multiple parties/coalitions for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Latvian Parliament. If a named coalition dissolves and does not contest this election, the corresponding market will resolve based on the number of seats won by the constituent party within that coalition that held the largest number of seats before the election.

If the results of this election are not known definitively by June 30, 2027 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Latvian government, specifically the Latvian Central Election Commission (https://www.cvk.lv/lv).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the Latvian Saeima election winner hinges on the neck-and-neck contest between LPV (36.5%) and JV (35.5%), mirroring latest polls like Factum's early October survey showing LPV at 21.8% and JV at 21.2% support amid a splintered field. This tightness persists due to LPV's rising populist momentum on sovereignty and welfare issues challenging JV's pro-EU incumbency edge, compounded by undecided voters (around 15%) and regional divides influencing Russian-speaking turnout. NA trails at 17.5% as a nationalist option. Separation could emerge from candidate announcements, debates, economic data releases, or stances on Ukraine aid ahead of the October 2026 vote.

question: Will the Prosperity Party (Prosperity) win the most seats in the 2026 Ethiopian general election?
description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Ethiopia on June 1, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Ethiopian House of Peoples' Representatives election. 

If the results are not known definitively by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Ethiopian Parliament.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Ethiopian government, specifically the National Election Board of Ethiopia (NEBE) (nebe.org.et/en).

eventMetadata.context_description: Prosperity Party's commanding 95.7% trader consensus for the Ethiopia parliamentary election winner stems from its 2021 landslide, capturing over 95% of seats amid opposition boycotts, regional insecurity, and institutional dominance under Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed. The party's control of electoral bodies, security apparatus, and the 2022 Pretoria peace deal with TPLF have reinforced stability and sidelined rivals like TPLF, EZEMA, GPDP, and NaMA. Fragmented opposition, ongoing Amhara conflicts, and economic pressures like inflation pose risks, but traders discount unified challenges or unrest derailing PP's incumbency advantage ahead of the next vote.

question: Will Bitcoin have the best performance in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the asset which has the best performance in 2026 among Bitcoin, Gold, and the S&P 500 with performance measured as the percentage change in price during the year for each asset. 

The percentage change in price for Bitcoin will be calculated by comparing the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT on January 1, 2026 12:00 AM ET to the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT on December 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET.

The resolution source for Bitcoin will be Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT in the chart with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar and the mouse on the candle for the relevant minute (the “time tools” selection may be used to view historical candles).

The percentage change in Gold will be calculated by comparing the official Gold Continuous Contract (GC00) Close price for the last trading day in 2025 to the official Gold Continuous Contract (GC00) Close price for the last trading day in 2026, as reported by MarketWatch.

The resolution source for Gold will be MarketWatch, specifically the close values reported for Gold Continuous Contract (GC00) under “Historical Quotes” at https://www.marketwatch.com/investing/future/gc00.

The percentage change in the S&P 500 will be calculated by comparing the official S&P 500 Index (^SPX) Close price for the last trading day in 2025 to the official S&P 500 Index (^SPX) Close price for the last trading day in 2026, as reported by Yahoo Finance.

The resolution source for the S&P 500 will be Yahoo Finance, specifically the Close values published by Yahoo Finance for S&P 500 Index (^SPX) at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5ESPX/history/.

If two or more listed assets have exactly the same performance for 2026, this market will resolve according to the asset whose name, as listed in the title of this market, comes first alphabetically (e.g. if Bitcoin and Gold tie, this market will resolve to Bitcoin).

Only closing prices will be used for all calculations; total return measures will not be applied.

If either of the relevant trading days are shortened, the official closing price published for that session will be used.

If any relevant day lacks a specified closing price, the last valid historical closing price offered by the resolution source will be used.

events.title: Bitcoin vs. Gold vs. S&P 500 in 2026
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the asset which has the best performance in 2026 among Bitcoin, Gold, and the S&P 500 with performance measured as the percentage change in price during the year for each asset. 

The percentage change in price for Bitcoin will be calculated by comparing the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT on January 1, 2026 12:00 AM ET to the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT on December 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET.

The resolution source for Bitcoin will be Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT in the chart with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar and the mouse on the candle for the relevant minute (the “time tools” selection may be used to view historical candles).

The percentage change in Gold will be calculated by comparing the official Gold Continuous Contract (GC00) Close price for the last trading day in 2025 to the official Gold Continuous Contract (GC00) Close price for the last trading day in 2026, as reported by MarketWatch.

The resolution source for Gold will be MarketWatch, specifically the close values reported for Gold Continuous Contract (GC00) under “Historical Quotes” at https://www.marketwatch.com/investing/future/gc00.

The percentage change in the S&P 500 will be calculated by comparing the official S&P 500 Index (^SPX) Close price for the last trading day in 2025 to the official S&P 500 Index (^SPX) Close price for the last trading day in 2026, as reported by Yahoo Finance.

The resolution source for the S&P 500 will be Yahoo Finance, specifically the Close values published by Yahoo Finance for S&P 500 Index (^SPX) at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5ESPX/history/.

If two or more listed assets have exactly the same performance for 2026, this market will resolve according to the asset whose name, as listed in the title of this market, comes first alphabetically (e.g. if Bitcoin and Gold tie, this market will resolve to Bitcoin).

Only closing prices will be used for all calculations; total return measures will not be applied.

If either of the relevant trading days are shortened, the official closing price published for that session will be used.

If any relevant day lacks a specified closing price, the last valid historical closing price offered by the resolution source will be used.

question: Ethereum all time high by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for ETH/USDT between 16 December '25 16:30 and 11:59PM ET on the date specified in the title has a final “High” price that is higher than any previous Binance 1 minute candle's "High" price on any prior date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT "High" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT with “1m” and “Candles” selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance ETH/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Will the All India Trinamool Congress (AITC) win the most seats in the 2026 West Bengal Legislative Assembly election?
description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in West Bengal, India, in March–April 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next West Bengal Legislative Assembly election. 

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the West Bengal Parliament. 

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Indian government, specifically the Election Commission of India (ECI) (eci.gov.in). If there are multiple reports, this market will resolve based on the one that includes the most Assembly Constituents (AC).

events.title: West Bengal Legislative Assembly Election Winner
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders strongly favor All India Trinamool Congress (AITC) at 75% implied probability for the West Bengal Legislative Assembly winner, anchored by its 2021 supermajority of 213 seats and dominant 2024 Lok Sabha results securing 29 of 42 seats, reinforcing regional incumbency advantages under Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee. Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) holds 25.5% as the primary challenger, buoyed by 2021 gains to 77 seats but tempered by subsequent bypoll losses and internal leadership frictions. Recent AITC sweeps in state by-elections like Ranaghat and Kaliganj have widened the gap, while fragmented opposition from INC, CPI(M), and others renders them negligible at under 0.1% each. Panchayat polls later this year may introduce volatility.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for SOL/USDT between 16 December '25 11:30 and 11:59PM ET on the date specified in the title has a final “High” price that is higher than any previous Binance 1 minute candle's "High" price on any prior date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the SOL/USDT "High" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/SOL_USDT with “1m” and “Candles” selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance SOL/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Will Trump pardon Keonne Rodriguez before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual receives a presidential pardon, commutation, or reprieve from Donald Trump between December 16, 2025 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for whether the individual is pardoned or not will be official information from the US government, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Who will Trump pardon before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual receives a presidential pardon, commutation, or reprieve from Donald Trump between November 17, 2025 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for whether the individual is pardoned or not will be official information from the US government, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President-elect Trump's post-election statements signaling intent to review and pardon non-violent January 6th defendants convicted in federal cases represent the dominant driver of trader consensus, with markets pricing high implied probabilities for that outcome before 2027. In a December Time magazine interview, Trump affirmed he would "absolutely" examine those cases, echoing his first-term use of clemency for allies like Roger Stone amid over 1,500 related convictions. Incoming Attorney General nominee Pam Bondi has voiced support for such reviews. His pardon authority activates upon January 20, 2025, inauguration, though exact targets, timing, and scope remain uncertain amid ongoing DOJ transitions and potential legal hurdles.

question: Trump suspends U.S. entry for more countries by by June 30?
description:
On December 16, 2025, President Donald Trump issued a proclamation titled “Restricting and Limiting the Entry of Foreign Nationals to Protect the Security of the United States”, which updated and expanded partial and full suspensions of entry into the U.S. for nationals of various countries (see: https://www.whitehouse.gov/presidential-actions/2025/12/restricting-and-limiting-the-entry-of-foreign-nationals-to-protect-the-security-of-the-united-states/). 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump issues any executive order, signs any bill into law, or if the Trump administration otherwise takes any formal action which is aimed at fully or partially suspending entry into the U.S. for nationals of any specific, additional country between market issuance and the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A qualifying action must be aimed at restricting or banning entry into the United States for nationals of a particular country or countries. Blanket actions to restrict entry for individuals holding certain visa classes, or other generalized entry restrictions, which do not target a specific country or countries, will not count.

Additional country refers to any country not subject to a full or partial suspension of entry as of the December 16 proclamation.

Any qualifying government action taken within this market’s timeframe will qualify for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of when the policy takes effect. However, announcements of future or planned bans which are not made official through government action will not qualify.

Any action meeting these criteria will count, even if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from the US federal government; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Transition officials like Stephen Miller have signaled plans to revive and expand Trump's first-term travel restrictions on Day One, targeting dozens of countries failing to repatriate deportees, driving trader consensus toward higher implied probabilities for new U.S. entry suspensions. This aligns with campaign pledges for aggressive immigration enforcement amid record border encounters. Past bans faced court blocks but were upheld after revisions, highlighting legal risks alongside diplomatic pushback potential. Uncertainty persists given implementation timelines and congressional oversight. Traders eye the January 20 inauguration and early executive orders as pivotal catalysts that could shift odds rapidly.

question: Will Pure Storage (PSTG) be added to the S&P 500 by March 31, 2026?
description:
The S&P 500 undergoes quarterly reconstitution on the third Friday of March, June, September, and December. The next rebalance is currently scheduled for March 20, 2026.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if S&P Global Inc. announces that the listed company will be added to the S&P 500 index by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

To qualify, the listed ticker must match the announced ticker added to the index.

An announcement from S&P will qualify for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether the listed company has actually been added to the S&P 500 index by the resolution date. 

The primary resolution source will be official announcements from S&P Global Inc. (https://www.spglobal.com/en/press/press-release).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Palantir (PLTR) at 35% implied probability and CrowdStrike (CRWD) at 28% for Q1 2026 S&P 500 additions, driven by their explosive market cap growth—PLTR surpassing $100B and CRWD at $85B—meeting the index's $20.5B threshold amid sustained GAAP profitability over the last four quarters. Recent rebalances, like December 2024's inclusion of Super Micro Computer, underscore the committee's preference for high-growth tech amid AI tailwinds, with liquidity and sector balance key factors. Upcoming Q4 2025 earnings (January-March 2026) represent pivotal catalysts, as misses could pivot odds toward alternatives like Trade Desk (TTD) or Datadog (DDOG), while broader Nasdaq rallies bolster growth stock viability against economic uncertainty.

question: Will the Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) win the most seats in the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary election?
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Slovenian National Assembly (Državni zbor) election. 

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Slovenian Parliament.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Slovenian government, specifically the Slovenian Election Authority (Valmyndigheten) (dvk-rs.si/).

Note: If a listed party contests the election as part of a jointly listed coalition with one or more parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the listed party will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If two or more listed parties contest the election as a jointly listed coalition, with or without additional parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the party within that coalition that won the most seats in the previous election will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If such a coalition dissolves, each market will again represent its respective party individually.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns a 95% implied probability to the Freedom Movement (GS) winning Slovenia's parliamentary election, driven by its persistent double-digit lead in the latest opinion polls from agencies like Mediana and Ninamedia, where GS holds 30-35% support compared to 20% for the Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS). This dominance reflects Prime Minister Robert Golob's coalition stability, effective handling of EU funds and infrastructure projects, and strong urban turnout projections following the 2022 victory. Recent catalysts include positive economic indicators and minimal scandals, reinforcing GS's frontrunner status. Realistic challenges include SDS rallying conservative voters on migration issues, surprise voter turnout surges, or last-minute coalition shifts among smaller parties like NSi or SD that could deny GS a majority.

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Slovenian Parliament.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Slovenian government, specifically the Slovenian Election Authority (Valmyndigheten) (dvk-rs.si/).

Note: If a listed party contests the election as part of a jointly listed coalition with one or more parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the listed party will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If two or more listed parties contest the election as a jointly listed coalition, with or without additional parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the party within that coalition that won the most seats in the previous election will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If such a coalition dissolves, each market will again represent its respective party individually.

question: Will Nicki Minaj advance from the 2026 California Governor primary election?
description:
The non-partisan primary election for Governor of California is scheduled to take place on June 2, 2026. The top two candidates in this election by number of votes won will advance to the general election for Governor of California.

This market will resolve to “Yes” If the listed candidate advances from the primary to the general election for Governor of California. Otherwise this market will resolve to “No”.

If no 2026 California gubernatorial primary takes place by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to “No.”

This market will resolve based on the results of the primary election for Governor of California as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the government of California, specifically the Office of the Secretary of State.

events.title: Who will advance from the California Governor primary?

eventMetadata.context_description: Katie Porter's lead in early polls, such as Emerson College's October survey showing her at 16% support, positions her as the frontrunner to advance from California's top-two gubernatorial primary on March 3, 2026, reflecting trader consensus on Polymarket where her implied probability exceeds 70%. With Governor Gavin Newsom term-limited, the open race features Democrats Tony Thurmond (12%), Eleni Kounalakis (11%), and Toni Atkins (9%) vying for the second spot, while Republicans like Riverside Sheriff Chad Bianco trail at 7-10% amid California's Democratic dominance. Porter's October campaign launch bolstered her momentum post-Senate bid, but fluid fields and undecided voters (over 50%) leave room for shifts. Key upcoming events include candidate filing deadlines in early 2025 and potential endorsements from Newsom or Harris allies.

question: Avatar 4 greenlit by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the fourth installment in James Cameron's Avatar series is explicitly announced to be in production or preparing to enter production by March 31, 2026 ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

To qualify for a "Yes" resolution, the film must be explicitly acknowledged by James Cameron, Lightstorm Entertainment, its distributor/studio (e.g., 20th Century Studios), or that distributor/studio's parent company (e.g., Disney) to be a sequel to "Avatar: Fire and Ash", and have a similar sequential relationship to the one between "Avatar: The Way of Water" and "Avatar: Fire and Ash". An announcement of a new qualifying film before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced film becomes available to the public. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from James Cameron, Lightstorm Entertainment, a qualifying film's distributor (e.g., 20th Century Studios) or parent company (e.g., Disney), or a consensus of credible sources.

events.title: Avatar 4 greenlit by March 31?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the fourth installment in James Cameron's Avatar series is explicitly announced to be in production or preparing to enter production by March 31, 2026 ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

To qualify for a "Yes" resolution, the film must be explicitly acknowledged by James Cameron, Lightstorm Entertainment, its distributor/studio (e.g., 20th Century Studios), or that distributor/studio's parent company (e.g., Disney) to be a sequel to "Avatar: Fire and Ash", and have a similar sequential relationship to the one between "Avatar: The Way of Water" and "Avatar: Fire and Ash". An announcement of a new qualifying film before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced film becomes available to the public. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from James Cameron, Lightstorm Entertainment, a qualifying film's distributor (e.g., 20th Century Studios) or parent company (e.g., Disney), or a consensus of credible sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors "No" at 85.5% implied probability for Avatar 4 receiving an official greenlight by March 31, primarily due to the absence of any formal announcement from Disney or Lightstorm Entertainment despite ongoing script refinements by James Cameron. While producer Jon Landau confirmed development on Avatar 4 and 5 last year, and principal photography wrapped on Avatar: Fire and Ash (slated for December 2025), studios typically require box office validation and finalized budgets before greenlighting sequels—precedent seen with the multi-year gap to Avatar 2. No leaks or teases emerged at recent events like CinemaCon, reinforcing skepticism amid Disney's packed slate of Marvel and Star Wars projects; traders eye potential D23 Expo updates as the key near-term catalyst.

question: XRP all time high by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for XRP/USDT between 16 December '25 11:30 and 11:59PM ET on the date specified in the title has a final “High” price that is higher than any previous Binance 1 minute candle's "High" price on any prior date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the XRP/USDT "High" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/XRP_USDT with “1m” and “Candles” selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance XRP/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for XRP/USDT between 16 December '25 11:30 and 11:59PM ET on the date specified in the title has a final “High” price that is higher than any previous Binance 1 minute candle's "High" price on any prior date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the XRP/USDT "High" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/XRP_USDT with “1m” and “Candles” selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance XRP/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Will Trump create a tariff dividend by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Trump Administration formally creates (e.g., by signing federal legislation or performing executive action) a tariff dividend/rebate by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Any bill signed into law or executive action taken within this market's time frame will qualify, regardless of when the law or action goes into effect.

A qualifying payment of any amount distributed to any segment of individual US taxpayers will qualify as long as it is clearly attributed primarily to tariff revenue rather than a routine tax refund or credit.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Will Trump create a tariff dividend by June 30?
events.description:
